data.title: LafargeHolcim Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 5.70, revenue of CHF 26.41B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• LafargeHolcim press release (OTCPK:HCMLF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 5.70.
  • Revenue of CHF 26.41B (-2.2% Y/Y).
  • Recurring EBIT of CHF 5,049m, +10.8% in local currency, +6.1% in CHF.
  • Free cash flow after leases of CHF 3,801 million, up 2.6%.
  • Proposed 11% increase in dividend to CHF 3.10.

  • Outlook and guidance

    Building on its record full-year performance, Holcim has a strong outlook across all of its business segments as follows:

    • North America: Infrastructure modernization and reshoring of manufacturing to drive growth in 2025
    • Latin America: Public and private sectors to drive infrastructure and commercial investments
    • Europe: Continued strong demand for sustainable building solutions expected to drive profitable growth
    • Asia, Middle East & Africa: Strong domestic demand in North Africa, positive outlook in Australia, price recovery in China
    • Solutions & Products: Favorable outlook for both new construction and repair & refurbishment market

    Well positioned for 2025, Holcim expects continued profitable growth with:

    • Mid-single digit Net Sales growth in local currency
    • Over proportional growth in recurring EBIT
    • Further expansion of recurring EBIT margin
    • Free cash flow of above CHF 3.5 billion
    • Continued double-digit growth in recycled construction demolition materials

data.title: ANI Pharmaceuticals beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ANI Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:ANIP): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.63 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $190.6M (+44.7% Y/Y) beats by $15.25M.
  • Adjusted non-GAAP EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $50.0 million, an increase of 65.7% from the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • As of December 31, 2024, the company had $144.9 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents, $221.7 million in net accounts receivable and $639.2 million in principal value of outstanding debt (inclusive of our senior convertible notes).
  • The company generated year-to-date cash flow from operations of $64.0 million.

data.title: WeRide participates in autonomous driving service for commercial use in France
data.description:
WeRide (NASDAQ:WRD) announced on Friday the first European fully driverless commercial deployment of its Robobus. The deployment was part of a shuttle service partnership with beti, Renault Group (OTCPK:RNSDF), and Macif that will feature an L4-level automated mobility service in France's Drôme region.

For the automated mobility service, WeRide (NASDAQ:WRD) will provide its L4-level autonomous technology for a passenger transport service on open roads in mixed traffic, with several six-meter-long robobuses, each able to carry eight passengers. Beti Automated Mobility will begin operations on March 10, at the Rovaltain business park – Valence TGV station in Drôme. The route is 3.3 km long and serves the train station, off-site long-term parking, the business park's catering hub, and 150 companies employing 3,000 people.

The initial learning phase for the automated mobility service will run from March 10 to April 19, operating Monday to Friday under real passenger transport conditions.

Shares of WeRide (WRD) were down 0.8% in premarket trading on Friday, after plunging 15.0% on Thursday. The robotaxi pure play stock is still up almost 40% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Chart Industries Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 misses by $0.49, revenue of $1.11B misses by $60M
data.description:
• Chart Industries press release (NYSE:GTLS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 misses by $0.49.
  • Revenue of $1.11B (+8.8% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • Orders of $1.55 billion, increased 29.4% and included phase one Woodside Louisiana LNG order
  • Our 2025 sales are anticipated to be in the range of $4.65 billion to $4.85 billion vs $4.68B consensus, associated anticipated adjusted EBITDA between $1.175 billion and $1.225 billion and associated adjusted diluted EPS of $12.00 to $13.00 vs $12.40 consensus on share count of approximately 45.5 million. We anticipate our tax rate will be approximately 22%. Additionally, we reiterate that we anticipate ending 2025 with approximately $3 billion of net debt, based on full year 2025 FCF generation between $550 and $600 million.

data.title: AES reports mixed Q4 earnings; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• AES press release (NYSE:AES): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $2.96B (-0.3% Y/Y) misses by $110M.

  • Guidance and Expectations:

    The Company is initiating 2025 guidance for Adjusted EBITDA of $2,650 to $2,850 million. Growth in 2025 is expected to be driven by contributions from new renewables projects, rate base growth at the Company's US utilities, and normalized results in Colombia and Mexico, partially offset by revenues from the monetization of the Warrior Run PPA in 2024 and asset sales.

    The Company is reaffirming its expectation for annualized growth in Adjusted EBITDA of 5% to 7% through 2027, from a base of its 2023 guidance of $2,600 to $2,900 million.

    The Company is initiating an expectation for 2025 Adjusted EBITDA with Tax Attributes of $3,950 to $4,350 million.

    The Company is initiating 2025 guidance for Adjusted EPS10 of $2.10 to $2.26 vs. $2.03 consensus. Growth in 2025 is expected to be primarily driven by contributions from new renewables projects, rate base growth at the Company's US utilities, and normalized results in Colombia and Mexico, partially offset by revenues from the monetization of the Warrior Run PPA in 2024, higher Parent interest, and a higher adjusted tax rate.

    The Company is reaffirming its annualized growth target for Adjusted EPS of 7% to 9% through 2025, from a base of 2020. The Company is also reaffirming its annualized growth target for Adjusted EPS of 7% to 9% through 2027, from a base of its 2023 guidance of $1.65 to $1.75.

    The Company's 2025 guidance is based on foreign currency and commodity forward curves as of December 31, 2024.

    The Company expects to maintain its current quarterly dividend payment of $0.17595 going forward.

data.title: China's Baidu plans to unveil AI model Ernie 4.5 on March 16 (Update)
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with a statement from Baidu.

Chinese internet search giant Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) plans to launch the next generation of its AI model, Ernie 4.5, with improved capabilities on March 16, the company said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

The Ernie 4.5 model will have improved capabilities in areas like reasoning and feature enhanced multimodal capabilities.

Multimodal AI refers to AI systems which are capable of processing and integrating information from different types of data such as text, video, images, and audio.

Baidu will open-source the ERNIE 4.5 model on June 30. The company will also provide its AI chatbot Ernie Bot free to all users starting April 1.

"ERNIE 4.5 will be our best model ever. And we would like to have our users and customers try them out more easily than before. So our decision to open source ERNIE 4.5 series, is also backed by our deep confidence in our technology leadership," CEO Robin Li had said during the company's fourth quarter earnings call on Feb. 18.

Earlier this month, it was reported that the company plans to launch AI model Ernie 5 in the second half of this year.

Baidu's efforts comes on the heels of Chinese AI startup DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) unveiling its AI model R1 last month and detailing in a paper how large language models, or LLMs, could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision. U.S. tech stocks plunged on Jan. 27, with Nvidia seeing around $589B in market cap erased on that day.

Li noted during the earnings call that one that thing the company learned from DeepSeek was that open sourcing the best models can greatly help adoption. "When the model is open source, people naturally want to try it out of curiosity, which helps drive broader adoption," Li added.

Earlier this month, domestic AI rival Alibaba (BABA) released a new family of AI models called Qwen2.5-VL. Meanwhile TikTok parent ByteDance (BDNCE) has its chatbot Doubao.

Baidu was among the first companies in China to launch its AI service in 2023 after Microsoft-backed OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT in late 2022. In November 2024, Li noted that the company's AI capabilities are gaining broader market recognition, due to the increasing adoption of its AI model Ernie.

Earlier this month, Li noted that investment in data centers and cloud infrastructure is still required in spite of the cost efficiency shown by DeepSeek compared to the large AI models. Li also said that "in China, because of the constraints of compute, we have to innovate to reduce the cost both on the inference side and the training side."
